review_title: Google Workspace a definate YES to use.
description: My overall experience I would classify as exceptional. It has made my workflow much more efficient and streamline.
author: Collette K.
pros: Google workspace is a great platform, from google sheets, google documents to many other available features. I utilize it mulitple times a day to perform my administrative tasks. Very user friendly. I prefer it over Microsoft word.
cons: There really are no Cons the only one I could even come up with is that one of my spreadsheets just disappeared and I tried everyway to recover it per customer service instruction and it was unable to be recovered. Did not appear in my drive or any other place on my computer

review_title: The best workplace software suite for any business
description: My business simply wouldn't be able to operate without it, and except for people who have exceptionally complex needs for Excel, it's hard for me to see how one could prefer Microsoft Office over Google Workspace. Google is much better designed, has a better UI, and strikes a better balance between sophistication and ease of use.
author: Justin C.
pros: I like how many different operations tools there are that are seamlessly integrated, and how well the Google Workspace Apps integrate with other third-party apps via API. I rely heavily on file sharing features for both internal operations and in our file-sharing with our clients, and it is easy for us and the customer to use.
cons: Sometimes finding the right place in settings can be difficult to find, and Google's help site is not always well organized due to the incredibly dense layers of different apps and systems on their platform.

description: Overall, we still use Google Workspace. Sometimes it's frustrating with the speed and capabilities. For a Small business its a great tool.
author: Andy N.
pros: You can use Google Workspace anywhere in the world. But the functionalities are minimal. It's great for a small business to keep all the documents in one place.
cons: Functionality and loading speed. It was very slow at times. Also, document editing tools don't have the capability as the desktop software like Office.

description: Love it! I think it's the leading workspace for tools for organisations across the board and always found it to be a stable solution.
author: Faz Y.
pros: Google workspace makes life easier, its super easy to use and comes with a great amount of cloud storage space too which is always helpful. The pricing plans are affordable too.
cons: It does miss some features relative to Microsoft such as some features on team or security of authentication. I also feel like the UI is a bit dated.

review_title: Good solution with lots application inside
description: A very good experience with this google solution wich provide lots of applications interconnected. It's a shame the price is too high to take advantage of all the possibilities.
author: Julien P.
pros: easy to setup. All applications are connected each other. Lot's off applications : docs, chat, mails, storage. A very large admin with plenty of possibility.
cons: A bit expansive for a small company. Admin iis not so easy to use. Price increase each few years. Need a high priced subscription to have a usable shared drive.

author: Brianna T.
pros: I like that I get a free Google voice number and that it allows me to have use my company's domain...
cons: It doesn't offer that much more (from what I can tell) than a regular Gmail account, and it can actually be confusing to navigate when you first sign up.

author: Sara D.
pros: It's imperative that my accounts are HIPAA compliant which is the reason I chose to utilize Google Workspace.
cons: Integrating the software with my existing website was a little bit challenging as I have limited knowledge of technology.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Connected, Reliable, and Easy to Use
description: My overall experience with Google Workspace has been excellent. It keeps all my tools and files connected, makes collaboration seamless, and helps me stay organized and productive every day.
author: jane F.
pros: I love how Google Workspace keeps everything connected and easy to use. Docs, Sheets, Calendar, and Gmail all work seamlessly together, making collaboration simple, saving time, and keeping my work organized.
cons: Sometimes managing multiple apps and shared files can feel a bit overwhelming, and certain features take time to learn, but overall it’s very reliable and efficient.

review_title: All‑in‑one communication and collaboration backbone for our property management team Overall rating
description: Overall, Google Workspace gives us a dependable, modern foundation for running our daily operations as a growing property management business. It keeps communication, documents, and scheduling under one roof, reduces reliance on IT support, and makes it easy for our team to work together from anywhere, which is why I strongly recommend it to other small and mid‑sized service or real estate companies.
author: Kartikeya G.
pros: The biggest pros are the tight integration between Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Meet, plus the fact that everything is cloud‑based and works well on any device. Email threads link directly into shared docs, calendar invites include Meet links by default, and our team can jump into real‑time editing on leases, reports, and checklists without dealing with version confusion. As an admin, I also appreciate the ability to manage users, aliases, groups, and security settings from a single console, which is especially helpful as we add contractors or new staff.
cons: There are a few areas that could be better, mainly around pricing and some advanced features. Pricing across tiers can be a bit confusing when deciding what level of storage and security we truly need, and upgrading specific power users to higher plans without changing everyone else would be helpful. A handful of advanced video‑conferencing or device‑management capabilities also sit behind higher tiers, which smaller teams may find difficult to justify.

description: I feel comfortable with Google Workspace and that comfort allows me to focus only only on the tasks at hand. I like the familiarity I have always had with Google and adding the Workspace as another tool feels like a natural and organic decision for me and my team!
author: Mai M.
pros: Our entire team is able to access Workspace on their desktop, tablet or phone with ease! We share files constantly throughout any given day and we will totally safe and secure when using Google Workspace to do so.
cons: Zero cons. I find Google Workspace the most user friendly platform of my entire suite of platforms. No notes, Google Workspace. You've nailed it!

review_title: One stop shop - full team collaboration
description: Always used outlook at PST data files for my company of 10 employees. The amount of tech support we needed with outgoing and incoming server issues, loss of emails and the mess we made whenever we had to set up a new compuyter and transfer over the employees previous data file. Workspace allowed us to set up all our emails in the cloud for access from anywhere. Also we were able to make distribution lists for general mail boxes for full visibility. All in all, its added a layer of collaboration to our workplace that has become extremely beneficial.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Was sick of transferring email data files for new computers, was losing data. Switched to Workspace and was able to organize and host all my companies email addreses, while also sharing calendars and forms. The thing I like most is that we can access our emails from anywhere using the gmail web app.
cons: The price is higher than what I was paying before but the peace of mind we get from the service warrants the spend.

description: Overall it's a great product for the business. I would recommend it to anyone. Great features that help make things easy and secure.
author: Emmanuel K.
pros: Great product. I like the entire platform, the design, cost and the capabilities. That is what we use for our company.
cons: It's pretty intuitive but it could be a bit to figure things out. Sometimes it's hard to get the support that might be needed from time to time.

review_title: Whether for Personal or Business, Google Workspace is my go-to
description: It has been very positive & I am glad I made the switch, it's very easy to use, share, collaborate, & integrate when needed
author: Katelynn F.
pros: I love Google Workspace because it has native integrations with pretty much every single digital platform or software available
cons: I sometimes face issues with syncing calendars, whether it's syncing calendars across different phones or syncing my Google calendar with Calendly

description: I have two small companies and the tools and apps in Google Workspace have helped me present them as solid corporations, with all the little things that great management and client service software can provide.
author: Sebastian B.
pros: For an amateur or a starting entrepreneur, this tool is a must. It provides you with everything you need to take your company from local, entry-level to a top corporation.
cons: Plans and subscription information may seem confusing, but Google’s team is ready to help you choose whatever is best for you, and guide you through the first steps which, if you are anything like me, will make you feel you need an IT degree to start with.

review_title: Google Workspace always meets my expectations!
description: My overall experience with Google Workspace has been very positive, and I can’t see myself using any other programs for documents.
author: Alyson P.
pros: have always been a big fan of Google’s and Google Drive's applications. The instant saving feature has always been why I use Google. I like the organization of the Google Workspaces platform and how I can personally sort my own documents, slides, and forms. The shared drive and calendar allow for efficient group workflow.
cons: My least favorite feature about Google Workspace and had to transfer all of our files from one main account and a secondary account. During the transfer, I found some information from the secondary account difficult to find, so I would go through and copy those files.

description: Overall, very happy with this product and would not switch to another platform. It has everything that we need and offers more services that we don't utilize but could in the future.
author: Ryan D.
pros: We’re a team of 21, and we use Google Workspace for a lot of our day-to-day work. It has made collaboration really easy. Being able to share documents, work in them at the same time, and track changes without things getting messy has been a big help. It is also just straightforward to use. People picked it up quickly without much training, which matters when you are running a small business and do not have time for complicated systems. For what we need, it feels cost-effective and does the job well.
cons: Organizing files and managing different folders or shared spaces can feel a bit clunky at times. It is not always as intuitive as it could be, especially as things grow and more people are involved. It may partly be user error on our end, but we do find ourselves occasionally having to search for files, resend links, or approve access requests. It is not a huge issue, but it can slow things down a bit and adds some extra steps here and there.

review_title: Necessary organizational Google tool simplifies communications
description: I zoom through my day with ease. I check my google emails quickly and respond timely using my AI tool to craft a response and schedule next steps video meetings and sent saved documents from my google drive.
author: Holly B.
pros: It is a robust platform of organized tools which are integrated with a AI component. I have a business email, google drive, can create Google Sheets, stay organized in a Google Drive, and set up my calendar with google video meetings.
cons: Nothing. It is very comprehensive and covers my organizational needs which is important in running my account management business role.

review_title: Seamless Collaboration and Real Time Document Editing
description: Provide satisfactory experience by making collaboration easier which allow teams on the same file in real time .Cloud based and multiple device access ensure files and emails are accessible from anywhere ,supporting both our remote and hybrid work.
author: Benoit Joyas S.
pros: The most favourite thing is it allow easy file sharing and seamless collaboration. Features like real time editing, simple file sharing and storage, cloud access and multi-device access allow our teams to work productively from any location. The tool is simple to use ,dependable and allow seamless integrations that minimizes the need for switching between multiple apps .Shared files access and smooth communication makes it easy and efficient to manage projects
cons: Google Workspace needs some improvements on areas like Google Sheet functionality where the tool lacks direct file upload unless we share links.

description: We utilize it with all of our clients and it has worked out great! The client and other team members can access what we have going on at the time.
author: Karen M.
pros: It is a great value for the money. It is easy to use and incorporates all aspects of communication. I like that we can add things to drives that only our clients can access with the options to view, edit or manage. I haven ran into any bugs or issues using the app and have never had to utilize the customer support. Which to me is a huge plus!
cons: At the present time I have no cons using Google Workspace. Would like to be able to add the Excel and docs from Microsoft easier, but not a big issue for me.

description: In my opinion Workspace becomes part of my daily routine, with Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and Meet working together in a way that feels natural and saves time. I often highlight real‑time collaboration, simple sharing, and an intuitive interface as reasons they “can’t imagine work without it” after a few years.
author: Sandra F.
pros: Real-time collaboration on Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Forms makes it very easy for teams to co-edit and comment from anywhere, which is especially helpful for remote or distributed work.​Fully cloud-based access means files, email, and calendars are available from any device with a browser or app, reducing version-control problems and simplifying remote work.​Tight integration across Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Meet, and other apps keeps communication, files, and scheduling in one ecosystem, which many users find intuitive and easy to manage.​Pricing is competitive for small and mid-size organizations, with per‑user plans that add storage, support, and admin features compared with free consumer Google accounts.​Security and admin controls (like Vault, endpoint management, and advanced security settings on higher tiers) support compliance and centralized management without a large IT team.​Strong search across Gmail and Drive makes it quick to find emails and files, improving day‑to‑day productivity
cons: What I liked least about Google Workspace was that the Browser-first apps can feel limited for power users who need very advanced spreadsheet, presentation, or desktop publishing features compared with full Microsoft Office desktop apps.​Full functionality assumes a stable internet connection; offline capabilities exist but are more limited and can be cumbersome for users who work frequently without connectivity.​Working with Microsoft Office files can cause formatting issues, especially with complex layouts or macros, which can frustrate teams collaborating with Office-heavy partners.​Lower-tier plans have relatively modest storage, and pooled storage models can require careful monitoring as organizations grow.​Some organizations find billing and licensing inflexible, such as needing all users on the same plan and having to manually adjust licenses when staff change, which can lead to unexpected costs.​Integration with some external calendaring and device-management tools is not always smooth, and admins sometimes report limitations with MDM and certain third‑party systems.

review_title: 4 Years Using Google Workspace in Real Estate
description: My overall experience with Google Workspace has been highly efficient and collaborative, especially in fast-paced environments where real-time communication and document sharing are essential. It stands out for how seamlessly it connects teams, allowing work to move forward without delays or version confusion. While it may not replace every advanced desktop tool, its simplicity, accessibility, and ability to keep everyone aligned make it a reliable and practical solution for day-to-day operations.
author: Adrian G.
pros: What I like most about Google Workspace is its seamless integration across applications, allowing for efficient collaboration and real-time updates within a unified ecosystem. The cloud-based architecture ensures accessibility, scalability, and automatic synchronization across devices without the need for complex infrastructure. Additionally, its built-in sharing controls and version history enhance transparency, data integrity, and team productivity.
cons: What I like least about Google Workspace is its occasional limitations in handling complex or large-scale data compared to more specialized desktop applications. Performance can sometimes lag with heavy files or multiple collaborators working simultaneously. Additionally, offline functionality and advanced feature depth can feel restricted in certain tools. Slow on MS Sharepoint integration.

review_title: Great space for team collaboration and document storage.
description: Google Workspace has been the right solution for our company. We are able to share docs, sheets, and forms between all of our branch offices seamlessly. It allows for overview of workspaces and the ability to share documents with our entire company.
author: Cordell S.
pros: I think Google Workspace is a superior team management workspace. It is extremely easy to navigate between the 20 shared drives that we have with each of our branch offices. I am able to move documents easily. Being able to use a special @ for emailing purposes makes team communication efficient. We have drives for "living" documents and drives for "finished" documents. Having drives for viewing only comes in really handy to make sure no one deletes or changes a "finished" document.
cons: I think the Google Admin workspace is a little difficult to navigate and could use a better UI. Adding or deleting users can be tricky.

description: Really no issues to worry about, new functions are always being added to make it flow and work better for my team.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We already use Google products, so the transition is easy. Clients are used to it so we can share files without problems, price is good. I have yet to even get close to running out of storage.
cons: I would like Gemini to help with managing my emails more, and I am looking forward to Google Drive connecting to out CRM, something that's been worked on for close to a year now.

author: Aleksandra T.
pros: Google Workspace is a comprehensive suite of collaboration and business productivity tools such as Google Sheets, Google Slides, Google Drive and Google Docs in one central repository.
cons: I have been using Google Workspace ever since 2019 and I have never noticed any flaw despite few bugs which come with new updates and are fixed within 24 hours.

description: Overall, I thoroughly enjoy and find all of the Google tools very useful, as I am constantly using them everyday. Google Workspace being able to connect to all of them, but also act as a planner, helps make it easier to get my job done.
author: Amber S.
pros: Google Workspace is a great tool that works as an integrated system with your Gmail, drive, etc.. It like that it is also helpful from a calendar perspective with scheduling and video conferencing.
cons: I’d say the biggest downfall to Google Workspace is the lack of customer support. I have had a very hard time trying to get a hold of someone when issues arise.
